perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor , or consultant.
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any employee, contractor , or consultant, but not its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that Euler Hermes is not liable for breaches of confidentiality by its Affiliates, contradicting the original agreement where Euler Hermes was liable for breaches by Affiliates. This leads to uncertainty about who is responsible for maintaining confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information only to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any employee, contractor , or consultant, but not its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that Euler Hermes is not liable for breaches of confidentiality by its Affiliates, while the contradicted text states that Euler Hermes is liable for breaches of confidentiality by its Affiliates. The explanation correctly identifies that this creates a direct contradiction regarding liability for breaches of confidentiality by Affiliates. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes will instruct its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants that may have access to the Confidential Information to keep it confidential in compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes will instruct its employees, contractors, or consultants that may have access to the Confidential Information to keep it confidential in compliance with this Agreement. Euler Hermes is not responsible to give instruction to its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that Euler Hermes is not responsible to give instruction to its Affiliates. In the previous sentence, it is understood that the Affiliates should keep it confidential. This adds an ambiguity where there is a confiential obligation, but no responsibility to give instructions on the confidentiallity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information from disclos ure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above, by using the same degree of care, but no less than reasonable care, that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature . Euler Hermes will instruct its employees, contractors, or consultants that may have access to the Confidential Information to keep it confidential in compliance with this Agreement. Euler Hermes is not responsible to give instruction to its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that Euler Hermes is not responsible for instructing its Affiliates regarding confidential information, while the contradicted text implies an obligation to protect confidential information from disclosure, which includes instructing relevant parties. This creates a direct conflict, as Euler Hermes is expected to safeguard confidential information but disclaims responsibility for instructing its Affiliates, who may have access to it. This represents an inconsistency as defined by the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall at all times remain the property of Company, and Euler Hermes has no rights to the Confidentia l Information except as specified in this Agreement. Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that inc orporates any of the Confidential Information , which shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement , nothwithstanding Paragraph 10 of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall at all times remain the property of Company, and Euler Hermes has no rights to the Confidentia l Information except as specified in this Agreement. Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that inc orporates any of the Confidential Information , which shall not remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement, notwithstanding Paragraph 10 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that any retained copies of Confidential Information shall not remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement, contradicting the original intention that all such material remains confidential. It undermines the confidentiality agreement by allowing Euler Hermes to retain and use information without any confidentiality restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information shall at all times remain the property of Company, and Euler Hermes has no rights to the Confidentia l Information except as specified in this Agreement. Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that inc orporates any of the Confidential Information , which shall not remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement, notwithstanding Paragraph 10 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the intention of the original agreement regarding confidentiality. The original text states that retained copies of confidential information remain subject to confidentiality obligations. The changed text explicitly states that these copies shall *not* remain subject to those obligations. The contradicted text is a duplicate of the changed text so there is a direct contradiction. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction as described by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. If the Confidential Information is no longer subject to confidentiality obligations, that directly conflicts with the original intention of the confidential agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Company makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Confidential Information and shall have no liability to Euler Hermes or its Affiliates as a result of the their use of the Confidential Information or as a result of any errors or omissions in the Confidential In formation .
perturbation.changed_text: Company makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Confidential Information. Company shall have liability to Euler Hermes or its Affiliates as a result of their use of the Confidential Information if the information is intentionally misleading .
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the company shall have liability if the information is intentionally misleading, while the original states the company has no liability in the Confidential Information. This creates uncertainty about whether the company is liable for the confidentiality information or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Confidential Information. Company shall have liability to Euler Hermes or its Affiliates as a result of their use of the Confidential Information if the information is intentionally misleading .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states the company has no liability for the confidential information, while the changed text states the company shall have liability if the information is intentionally misleading. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the company's liability, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating that the modification itself creates the contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall expire at the end of five (5) years from the date set forth below , and may be renewed upon mutual written consent of Euler Hermes and the Company .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall expire at the end of ten (10) years from the date set forth below , and may be renewed by Euler Hermes only .
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement shall expire at the end of ten (10) years, while the original states five (5) years, and that it may be renewed by Euler Hermes only, while the original states Euler Hermes and the company. This makes it unclear when the agreement ends, and who can renew it, leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall expire at the end of ten (10) years from the date set forth below , and may be renewed by Euler Hermes only .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement's expiration term from five (5) years to ten (10) years and alters the renewal condition from mutual written consent to Euler Hermes only. The contradicted text also states that 'This Agreement shall expire at the end of ten (10) years from the date set forth below, and may be renewed by Euler Hermes only .'. The original text stated that the agreement shall expire at the end of five (5) years from the date set forth below, and may be renewed upon mutual written consent of Euler Hermes and the Company. This directly contradicts the new text and contradicted text. The modification of the expiration period and renewal condition creates a clear inconsistency within the document and therefore the answer is YES based on the definition of inconsistencies.
